title: UKHSA Presents
body: " How to join UKHSA Presents  UKHSA Presents is open for any of our stakeholders or partners with an interest to attend. All the materials from each webinar will be added to this page. Sign up to our Stakeholder Cascade to receive a weekly newsletter with the latest events, news, updates and guidance from UKHSA.  Sign up   Next event  Details of the next UKHSA Presents webinar will be published shortly.  Previous events  October 2025: Radiation protection - Research, response and real life  On Thursday 23 October, we examined how UKHSA protects public health from radiation threats across the country. The session showcased UKHSA’s emergency response capabilities and core radiation protection services, drawing on examples from recent training exercises and real incidents to illustrate how specialist teams deliver coordinated responses during radiation emergencies.  Participants learnt about UKHSA’s partnership with the newly established Health Protection Research Unit (HPRU) on Radiation Threats and Hazards, and how this research collaboration enhances protective measures. We also discussed public engagement work, including the recently published ‘Living with Radiation’ document, and reviewed approaches to communicating complex radiation risks that help communities make informed decisions about radiation in everyday life.  Watch the recording (closed captions are available):  Radiation protection - Research, response and real life   Download the slidedeck:            UKHSA Presents: Radiation protection - Research, response and real life    PDF, 4.21 MB, 31 pages     This file may not be suitable for users of assistive technology.    Request an accessible format.    If you use assistive technology (such as a screen reader) and need a version of this document in a more accessible format, please email publications@ukhsa.gov.uk. Please tell us what format you need. It will help us if you say what assistive technology you use.      July 2025: The power of vaccine development and global partnerships  On Thursday 10 July, we dived into how the UKHSA’s Vaccine Development and Evaluation Centre (VDEC) was at the forefront of accelerating vaccine innovation. The webinar explored VDEC’s robust scientific capabilities, showcasing its vital contribution to global health security. Featuring contributions from the Oxford Vaccine Group and the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI), the session demonstrated how VDEC’s partnerships were instrumental in addressing global health challenges and strengthening vaccine preparedness.  Watch the recording (closed captions are available):  The power of vaccine development and global partnerships  Download the slidedeck:            UKHSA Presents: The power of vaccine development and global partnerships    PDF, 4.47 MB, 34 pages     This file may not be suitable for users of assistive technology.    Request an accessible format.    If you use assistive technology (such as a screen reader) and need a version of this document in a more accessible format, please email publications@ukhsa.gov.uk. Please tell us what format you need. It will help us if you say what assistive technology you use.      May 2025: AI Application in Health Security  On Thursday 8 May, we showcased UKHSA’s AI developments, demonstrated real-world applications already enhancing health security and discussed future possibilities and ethical considerations. Attendees discovered how UKHSA has been responsibly harnessing AI’s power to stay ahead of emerging threats, while navigating the complex balance of innovation and risk.  Watch the recording (closed captions are available):  UKHSA Presents: AI Application in Health Security  Download the slidedeck:             UKHSA Presents: AI Application in Health Security    PDF, 3.68 MB, 35 pages     This file may not be suitable for users of assistive technology.    Request an accessible format.    If you use assistive technology (such as a screen reader) and need a version of this document in a more accessible format, please email publications@ukhsa.gov.uk. Please tell us what format you need. It will help us if you say what assistive technology you use.      February 2025: mSCAPE Genomics  On Wednesday 19 February, our UKHSA Presents webinar explored UKHSA’s groundbreaking work in the use of pathogen genomics.  Our panel of speakers focused on the world-first metagenomics Surveillance Collaboration and Analysis Programme (mSCAPE), which has been developed to aid in the rapid detection of infectious diseases that could threaten the UK.  Watch the recording (closed captions are available):  UKHSA Presents: Pathogen genomics  Download the slidedeck:             mSCAPE Genomics    PDF, 1.39 MB, 17 pages     This file may not be suitable for users of assistive technology.    Request an accessible format.    If you use assistive technology (such as a screen reader) and need a version of this document in a more accessible format, please email publications@ukhsa.gov.uk. Please tell us what format you need. It will help us if you say what assistive technology you use.      November 2024: Antimicrobial Resistance  On Thursday 14 November, the second webinar in our series explored UKHSA’s work on tackling antimicrobial resistance (AMR).  Our panel of speakers discussed the challenges of AMR and UKHSA’s work on health surveillance, tracking and analysing resistance, developing evidenced-based interventions and raising public awareness.  Watch the recording (closed captions are available):  Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR)  Download the slidedeck:             Antimicrobial resistance    PDF, 5.06 MB, 31 pages     This file may not be suitable for users of assistive technology. September 2024: Vector-borne diseases  On Thursday 5 September 2024, we held the first edition of UKHSA Presents on vector-borne diseases.
